proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7); Tier M cap is appropriate but re-evaluate if D-8 revises exit policy
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio has no triggered concentration (0.7333 below 0.80) and only 0 Bear-regime exposure, so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-05) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Regime concentration observed 0.7333 is close to the 0.80 trigger; if Choppy concentration continues rising, downstream promotions on active cohorts should be scrutinized (Bear-gated promotion here is not affected).
R-multiple 0.98 near Tier S threshold suggests cohort could shift tier on next archive; demotion auto-fires per §3 if drift occurs.
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and no regime concentration is triggered (observed 0.7333 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-05) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (86.7%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits; prefer diversifying into non-UP_TRI setups (e.g., mean-reversion or short-side proxies) if any qualify
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A regime flip to Bear or a sharp Bull-to-Choppy transition would hit the entire book simultaneously since 100% of positions are directional long on unvalidated Candidate-tier cohorts — no offsetting exposure to buffer drawdown

evidence: UP_TRI share = 13/15 = 86.67%, above the 0.85 signal-type threshold
Book is fully directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 15/15 = 100%)
All four concentrated cells (UP_TRI×Bull×Pharma, ×FMCG, ×Other, and UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma) match only Candidate-tier cohorts — unvalidated edge
Regime concentration (Bull 73.33%) within tolerance but skewing directional
No single cell breaches 0.20 (top four cells tied at 13.33%)
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits; prefer diversifying into non-UP_TRI setups (e.g., mean-reversion or short-side proxies) if any qualify
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A regime flip to Bear or a sharp Bull-to-Choppy transition would hit the entire book simultaneously since 100% of positions are directional long on unvalidated Candidate-tier cohorts — no offsetting exposure to buffer drawdown
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 13/15 = 86.7% (above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Bull×UP_TRI and Choppy×UP_TRI cohorts for the concentrated cells sit only at Candidate tier — unvalidated edge. Regime concentration (Bull 73.3%) and individual cells (max 13.33%) are within tolerance, but the signal-type monoculture on unvalidated cohorts is the actionable risk.
